title: Leader of an Array

content.problemStatement:
Write a program to print all the **leaders** in the array. An element is a leader if it is strictly greater than all the elements to its right side. And the rightmost element is always a leader.


Input Format

The first line contains NNN, the number of elements in an array.
The second line contains NNN integers, denoting the elements in the array.


Output Format
In a single line output all the leaders in the given array.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N, the size of the array. The second line contains N space‑separated integers denoting the array elements.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing all leaders of the given array in the order they appear from left to right, separated by a single space.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Scanning from right, leaders are 2, 5, 17. Reversing gives left‑to‑right order 17 5 2.
All elements are equal; only the rightmost element qualifies as a leader.
Array is strictly increasing; only the last element has no greater element on its right.

solution.approach: Scanning the array from right to left keeps track of the maximum element seen so far. The rightmost element is always a leader. While moving left, an element is a leader iff it is strictly greater than the current maximum; when it is, update the maximum and record the element. The recorded leaders are in reverse order, so they are printed after reversing to obtain left‑to‑right order. This runs in O(N) time per test case with O(N) auxiliary space for storing leaders.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) for all test cases combined.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) auxiliary space for the list of leaders (can be O(1) if printed on the fly).

tcGeneratorPython:
def generate_case(i, RINT, POWER):
    # i: test case index (0‑based) up to 20
    # RINT(a,b): random integer in [a,b]
    # POWER(a,b): a**b
    if i == 0:
        print(1)
        print(1)
        print(0)
    elif i == 1:
        print(1)
        print(1)
        print(10000)
    elif i == 2:
        print(1)
        print(2)
        print('0 0')
    elif i == 3:
        print(1)
        print(2)
        print('5 1')
    elif i == 4:
        print(1)
        n = 15
        print(n)
        arr = [10000 - j for j in range(n)]
        print(' '.join(map(str, arr)))
    elif i == 5:
        print(1)
        n = 15
        print(n)
        print(' '.join(['10000']*n))
    elif i == 6:
        print(1)
        n = 15
        print(n)
        arr = []
        for j in range(n):
            arr.append('10000' if j%2==0 else '0')
        print(' '.join(arr))
    elif i == 7:
        print(1)
        n = 15
        print(n)
        print(' '.join(map(str, range(n))))
    elif i == 8:
        print(1)
        n = 5
        print(n)
        print(' '.join(['7']*n))
    elif i == 9:
        print(1)
        n = 8
        print(n)
        print(' '.join(['123']*n))
    elif i == 10:
        print(1)
        n = 5
        print(n)
        print('1 2 3 4 5')
    elif i == 11:
        print(1)
        n = 5
        print(n)
        print('5 4 3 2 1')
    elif i == 12:
        print(1)
        n = 6
        print(n)
        print('2 3 5 7 11 13')
    elif i == 13:
        print(1)
        n = 5
        print(n)
        print('9999 10000 9998 10000 9997')
    elif i == 14:
        print(1)
        n = 3
        print(n)
        print('5 5 6')
    elif i == 15:
        print(1)
        n = 4
        print(n)
        print('0 10000 0 10000')
    elif i == 16:
        print(3)
        print(2)
        print('1 2')
        print(3)
        print('3 2 1')
        print(4)
        print('4 4 4 4')
    elif i == 17:
        print(2)
        print(5)
        print('1 2 3 4 5')
        print(5)
        print('5 4 3 2 1')
    elif i == 18:
        print(5)
        for val in [7,8,9,10,11]:
            print(1)
            print(val)
    elif i == 19:
        print(1)
        n = 15
        print(n)
        print('3 1 4 1 5 9 2 6 5 3 5 8 9 7 9')
